## Ready-to-Cite Claims

Drop these into a blog verbatim — they are pre-computed from the live corpus and are safe to quote. Re-run this generator before citing to ensure freshness.

- Across 292 published personality profiles on 9takes, Enneagram types are not evenly distributed — Type 3 (Achiever) is the most common at 19.9% of the corpus.
- Among 65 actors profiled on 9takes, Type 9 (Peacemaker) is over-represented at 15.4% — +6.14 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 49 musicians profiled on 9takes, Type 4 (Individualist) is over-represented at 36.7% — +23.38 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 68 creators & influencers profiled on 9takes, Type 3 (Achiever) is over-represented at 29.4% — +9.55 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 31 politicians profiled on 9takes, Type 2 (Helper) is over-represented at 19.4% — +11.14 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 43 tech & business founders profiled on 9takes, Type 5 (Investigator) is over-represented at 20.9% — +14.42 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 23 comedians profiled on 9takes, Type 7 (Enthusiast) is over-represented at 39.1% — +24.75 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 12 authors & journalists profiled on 9takes, Type 5 (Investigator) is over-represented at 25.0% — +18.49 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 13 historical figures profiled on 9takes, Type 4 (Individualist) is over-represented at 30.8% — +17.41 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- The 263 graded profiles on 9takes average 8.83/10 on our internal quality rubric (originality, evidence, writing, hook).
- 88.7% of our published profiles have been refreshed in the last 90 days (259 of 292).

---

## Methodology

- **Source:** `blogs_famous_people` Supabase table. One row per profiled public figure.
- **Scope of public-facing stats:** `published = true` rows only. Unpublished drafts are excluded from all percentages and over/under-representation deltas.
- **Domain buckets:** Raw `type` labels are grouped into readable categories (e.g. `movieStar` + `newMovieStar` + `actor` → Actors). Domains with fewer than 10 profiled figures are omitted to avoid small-sample noise.
- **Over/under-representation:** Each domain's type share minus the corpus-wide baseline share, in percentage points. Positive means over-represented vs. the 9takes corpus average, not vs. general population.
- **Grading:** `content_quality.overall` is 9takes' internal 10-point rubric covering originality, evidence, writing, and hook. Not all profiles are graded.
- **Multi-domain figures:** A person tagged with both `musician` and `activist` is counted in both domains.

_Regenerate with `pnpm gen:corpus-stats`. Refresh cadence: monthly._
